## Summary

Expands the Reliability / Editorial Doctrine page from a compact trust page into a fuller doctrine page about evidence, restraint, scope discipline, and author sovereignty.

## Why

The deficiency roadmap calls out Reliability / Editorial Doctrine as too thin. It needs stronger sections on evidence over taste, diagnosis before polish, structure before sentence repair, author sovereignty, AI as instrument not authority, no blind rewriting, and scope discipline.

## Changes

- Reframes the page as **Reliability · Editorial Doctrine**.
- Adds the core line: **Evidence, not authority. Proposals, not control.**
- Expands trust principles:
  - Manuscript Sovereignty
  - Evidence Before Authority
  - Author-in-the-Loop
  - Scope Discipline
- Adds “What reliability means in practice” with:
  - Evidence over taste
  - Diagnosis before polish
  - Structure before sentence repair
  - AI as instrument, not authority
  - No blind rewriting
  - Voice protection as a constraint
- Adds a scope-discipline section showing why the wrong intervention can make a manuscript worse.
- Adds a may / must-not contract so RevisionGrade can recommend without seizing the pen.
- Adds a reliability sequence: Evaluate → Diagnose → Constrain → Decide.
- Expands boundaries and links the page to Black Box, Methodology, Privacy Controls, Security, and Revise.

## Scope

Public copy/page structure only.

No auth, database, evaluation, report, Revise runtime, TrustedPath runtime, Storygate runtime, Agent Readiness runtime, or pipeline behavior changed.
